description: Neuro-linguistic programming (NLP) and persuasion techniques repurposed for harm. Paraphrased descriptions only; NOT verbatim from any copyrighted work. Pattern recognition, not instruction.

entries.description: Associating a target's emotional state with a specific trigger to later recall that emotion on demand, bypassing their rational evaluation.
keywords: every time you hear this word
I'll remind you of this feeling
entries.source: Bandler & Grinder (1979) Frogs into Princes. Paraphrased. Pattern recognition only.

entries.description: Presenting someone with two choices where both options serve the manipulator's interests and neither respects the target's autonomy.
keywords: either you do X or you prove you don't care
entries.source: Bateson et al. (1956) on double-bind communication; Watzlawick (1967). Paraphrased.

entries.description: Embedding false assumptions in questions or statements so the target accepts those assumptions implicitly when answering.
keywords: now that you know the truth
entries.source: Chomsky transformational grammar; Lakoff (1975) on presupposition. Paraphrased.

entries.description: Reinterpreting an objectionable act in a flattering or neutral frame to make the target accept it without recognising the harm.
keywords: it's not lying it's protecting you
it's not stealing it's taking back what's mine
entries.source: Cialdini (1984) reframing principle (paraphrased). Applied to harmful contexts.

entries.description: Building rapport by mirroring the target's current state, then gradually leading them toward a conclusion or behaviour they would not otherwise choose.
keywords: I know exactly how you feel
and that is why you should
entries.source: Bandler & Grinder (1979). Paraphrased pattern description only.

entries.description: Deliberately overloading someone with contradictory information or rapid subject changes to induce a compliant state in which critical thinking is bypassed.
entries.source: Milton Erickson hypnotic induction patterns (academic description). Paraphrased.

entries.description: Claiming or implying expertise, credentials, or institutional backing that does not exist in order to suppress critical questioning.
